---
title: "The research workflow"
toc: true
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.kicker}
APPENDIX · RESEARCH WORKFLOW
:::
# The research workflow
::: {.lead}
Nine stages from a research question to knowledge that survives
replication.
:::
:::

Statistics is a thread running through a much longer process. The diagram
below names the stages of that process and links each to the parts of
the curriculum that cover it in detail. No stage is optional; errors
made at one are paid for at the next.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Q[1. Question] --> M[2. Measurements]
  M --> D[3. Design]
  D --> A[4. Acquisition]
  A --> Desc[5. Description]
  Desc --> An[6. Analysis]
  An --> I[7. Interpretation / Prediction]
  I --> V[8. Validation]
  V --> K[9. Knowledge / Decisions]
```

## 1. Question

A well-formed research question has a population, an exposure or
intervention, a comparator, an outcome, and a time frame. The PICO
framework from evidence-based medicine is a useful checklist.
A vague question leads to a vague design and a vague answer; a
well-formed question survives peer review before a single datum is
collected.
*Covered in:* [Course 1 W1 S1](../chapters/01-foundations/labs/lab-scientific-process-and-research-workflow.qmd),
[Course 3 W4 S1](../chapters/15-metaanalysis/labs/lab-systematic-reviews-and-prisma.qmd).

## 2. Measurements

Every question depends on the measurement scale you choose to answer it.
Accuracy, precision, reliability, and the difference between a measurement
and a proxy are the vocabulary.
*Covered in:* [Course 1 W1 S3](../chapters/01-foundations/labs/lab-data-types-tidy-data-accuracy-and-precision.qmd),
[Course 2 W4 S2](../chapters/14-clinical/labs/lab-kappa-icc-bland-altman.qmd).

## 3. Design

Design is where you choose what the data can and cannot tell you. A
well-designed observational study answers some questions better than a
badly designed RCT; a good RCT answers them all.
*Covered in:* [Course 3 W1](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-observational-designs-and-strobe.qmd).

## 4. Acquisition

Getting the data into a computer faithfully is itself a statistical
problem: missingness, measurement error, and batch effects start here.
*Covered in:* [Course 1 W1 S4](../chapters/01-foundations/labs/lab-import-joins-and-missingness-with-dplyr.qmd),
[Course 3 W2](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-mcar-mar-mnar.qmd).

## 5. Description

Before any inference, look at the data. A well-designed plot is often
the whole answer.
*Covered in:* [Course 1 W1 S5](../chapters/06-visualisation/labs/lab-ggplot2-grammar-and-multi-panel-layouts.qmd),
[Course 1 W2 S1](../chapters/02-descriptives/labs/lab-descriptive-statistics-and-table-1.qmd).

## 6. Analysis

The formal model that takes the data and returns an estimate, an
interval, and a decision.
*Covered in:* Courses 1–4, throughout.

## 7. Interpretation / prediction

What does the estimate mean in the scale of the science? Effect size,
clinical significance, and the distinction between an average and an
individual prediction live here.
*Covered in:* [Course 2 W3 S5](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-calibration-discrimination-roc-auc-brier-score.qmd),
[Course 4 W3 S5](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-time-dependent-brier-ipa-external-validation.qmd).

## 8. Validation

Does the finding hold in data not used to find it? External validation,
nested CV, and replication pool here.
*Covered in:* [Course 4 W1 S1](../chapters/13-ml/labs/lab-cross-validation-nested-cv-bootstrap-632.qmd),
[Course 4 W3 S5](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-time-dependent-brier-ipa-external-validation.qmd).

## 9. Knowledge / decisions

The act of writing it down so that others can trust, cite, and build on
the finding.
*Covered in:* [Writing a report](D-writing-a-report.qmd),
[Course 2 W4 S5](../reporting/lab-explanation-vs-prediction-reporting.qmd).
